beginner.title: Can & Can't – Ability & Permission
beginner.content:
📌 Khi nào dùng CAN / CAN'T
Can được dùng để diễn tả:
• Khả năng (ability): I can swim. She can't cook.
• Cho phép (permission): You can sit here. You can't smoke here.
📐 Công thức
LoạiCông thứcVí dụ
Khẳng địnhS + can + VI can speak English.
Phủ địnhS + can't + VI can't dance.
Câu hỏiCan + S + V?Can you swim?
Trả lời ngắnYes / No + S + can/can'tYes, I can. / No, I can't.
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ I can to swim. → ✅ I can swim. (không có "to")
❌ He can't rides a bike. → ✅ He can't ride a bike. (can't + V nguyên thể)
❌ Can you to help me? → ✅ Can you help me? (không có "to")
Can you play the piano? – No, I can't, but I can play the guitar.Bạn có chơi được piano không? – Không, nhưng tôi chơi được guitar.
I'm sorry, but you can't park here — it's a private area.Xin lỗi, bạn không được đỗ xe ở đây — đây là khu vực riêng.
She can speak three languages — English, French, and Spanish.Cô ấy có thể nói ba thứ tiếng — Anh, Pháp và Tây Ban Nha.

exercises.q: I ___ swim very well, but I'm learning.
___ you drive a car? I need someone to take me to the airport.
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi về khả năng → Can + S + V?

exercises.q: He ___ speak Japanese — he learned it when he lived in Tokyo.
I'm really sorry but I ___ come to your party on Saturday.
___ you ___ help me with this heavy box, please?
exercises.opts: Can / can,Could / could,Do / can,Are / able to
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi lịch sự → Can + S + V?

exercises.q: She ___ ride a horse — she does it every weekend.
We ___ go to the cinema tonight — the new film is fantastic!
I'm afraid you ___ use your phone during the exam — it's against the rules.
The children ___ play outside now because the weather is nice.
I ___ understand what he's saying — he's speaking too fast.
This restaurant ___ seat fifty people at once — it's quite large.
___ I ___ borrow your umbrella? It's starting to rain outside.
exercises.opts: Can / borrow,Could / borrow,May / to borrow,Do / borrow
exercises.exp: Xin phép lịch sự → Can + S + V?

exercises.q: She ___ speak two languages — that's quite an impressive skill!
We ___ leave early because the traffic is getting worse.
You ___ bring your pet inside this building — it's not allowed.
The baby ___ walk yet — she's only nine months old.
Can you to swim? → Correct or Wrong?
I can't speak Chinese — it's too difficult for me. → Correct or Wrong?
She can to play the violin beautifully. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: She can play the violin

exercises.q: We can go to the beach today — the sun is shining. → Correct or Wrong?
___ (can) you ___ (cook) Italian food? I love pasta!
I'm sorry but I ___ (can/not) ___ (come) to your birthday party.
She ___ (can) speak three languages — English, Spanish, and French.
___ (can) I ___ (use) your phone to make a quick call?
The baby ___ (can/not) ___ (walk) yet — she can only crawl.
We ___ (can) ___ (see) the mountains from our hotel room.
He ___ (can/not) ___ (remember) where he left his keys.
___ (can) you ___ (help) me carry these bags, please?
You ___ (can/not) ___ (park) here — this is a private car park.
I ___ (can) ___ (understand) what she's saying — she speaks very fast.
They ___ (can/not) ___ (come) tonight — they have to work late.
___ (can) we ___ (sit) here while we wait for the train?
My grandfather ___ (can) ___ (tell) amazing stories about his life.
You ___ (can/not) ___ (smoke) in this building — it's completely forbidden.
___ (can) she ___ (drive)? She has a licence but she's nervous.
I ___ (can) ___ (hear) something — do you hear that strange noise?
We ___ (can/not) ___ (go) out today because it's raining heavily.
___ (can) it ___ (be) your turn next after me — I'm almost done.
She ___ (can) ___ (solve) this puzzle — she's very good at them.
You ___ (can/not) ___ (take) photos inside the museum — it's not allowed.
___ (can) you ___ (open) the window? It's getting very hot in here.

reorder.words: I can speak English fluently
Can you help me with this problem
She can't swim very well yet
We can go to the beach today
Can I use your telephone please
He can't ride a bicycle
They can play the piano beautifully
Can you speak Japanese
I can't understand what you mean
She can cook amazing Italian food
Can we sit here for a while
The children can't reach the shelf
We can meet at six o'clock
Can I borrow your umbrella
He can't come to the party tonight
I can see the mountains from here
Can you hear that strange noise
She can't decide which dress to wear
We can finish this by Friday
Can I bring my friend to the event

passage.text: Hi! My name is Tom and I {{1}} (can/tell) you about my new job. I work in a busy restaurant in the city centre. I {{2}} (can/work) six days a week, which is quite tiring, but I {{3}} (can/enjoy) meeting lots of different people. I {{4}} (can/not/cook) the food, but I {{5}} (can/serve) the customers and {{6}} (can/clean) the tables. The manager says I {{7}} (can/learn) new skills quickly, which is good. I {{8}} (can/not/sleep) well after late shifts, but I {{9}} (can/get up) early every morning. In the future, I {{10}} (can/become) a manager myself if I work hard. My parents say I {{11}} (can/be) proud of myself for finding a job.

Welcome to our language school! I {{1}} (can/welcome) you and {{2}} (can/tell) you about our courses. You {{3}} (can/not/choose) the time of your classes, but you {{4}} (can/decide) which level to start at. Our teachers {{5}} (can/help) you improve your English quickly. You {{6}} (can/not/bring) food inside the classrooms. You {{7}} (can/use) our library and computers every day. We {{8}} (can/offer) speaking practice sessions every Wednesday. You {{9}} (can/not/smoke) anywhere in the building. At the end of the course, you {{10}} (can/take) an exam to get a certificate. I hope you {{11}} (can/enjoy) learning with us!

My sister is learning to drive. She {{1}} (can/not/drive) yet, but she {{2}} (can/pass) the theory test soon. She {{3}} (can/go) to driving lessons every Saturday morning. The instructor says she {{4}} (can/learn) quickly if she practises more. She {{5}} (can/not/afford) driving lessons every week — it's quite expensive. I {{6}} (can/let) her practise with my car in an empty car park. She {{7}} (can/not/drive) on real roads yet — she needs more practice. I hope she {{8}} (can/pass) her test next month. Then we {{9}} (can/go) on road trips together! She says she {{10}} (can/be) a good driver if she tries hard.

Hello! My name is Anna and I'm going to tell you about my abilities. I {{1}} (can/speak) three languages — English, German, and Russian. I {{2}} (can/not/play) any musical instruments, though I wish I could. I {{3}} (can/cook) delicious meals for my family. I {{4}} (can/not/swim) — it's something I need to learn. I {{5}} (can/write) stories and poems in my free time. I {{6}} (can/not/run) very fast, but I'm good at yoga. I {{7}} (can/understand) people's feelings easily. I {{8}} (can/not/remember) names very well — it's my weakness. In the future, I hope I {{9}} (can/learn) to play the piano. I want to be someone who {{10}} (can/do) many different things.

Welcome to our fitness centre! Here, you {{1}} (can/choose) from many different activities. You {{2}} (can/do) yoga, pilates, or weight training. You {{3}} (can/not/bring) your own food to eat in the gym. You {{4}} (can/use) our changing rooms and showers. You {{5}} (can/not/enter) the gym after ten o'clock at night. Personal trainers {{6}} (can/help) you create a fitness plan. You {{7}} (can/not/smoke) anywhere in the building. You {{8}} (can/book) classes online in advance. We {{9}} (can/offer) a discount for yearly memberships. I hope you {{10}} (can/reach) your fitness goals here! You {{11}} (can/be) as healthy as you want to be.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những điều bạn có thể và không thể làm dùng can/can't.
writing_prompts.example: I can speak two languages — English and Vietnamese. I'm learning to speak a third language, maybe Japanese. I can cook simple meals like pasta and fried rice. I can't cook complicated dishes yet. I can swim fairly well, but I can't swim very long distances. I can play the guitar a little, but I can't play professionally. I wish I could play the piano.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về khả năng của một người bạn hoặc thành viên gia đình dùng can/can't.
writing_prompts.example: My best friend can speak four languages — it's amazing! She can also paint beautiful pictures and play the violin. She can't sing very well, but she can dance brilliantly. My brother can fix anything — he has natural talent with tools. He can't cook at all though — everything he makes tastes terrible! My mother can speak three languages, but she can't swim.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những điều bạn được phép và không được phép làm ở một nơi nào đó.
writing_prompts.example: At our school, you can use the library every day after class. You can use computers for your school projects. You can't eat inside the classrooms — you need to go to the canteen. You can't run in the corridors — it's dangerous. You can bring your own water bottles. You can't bring smartphones to class — they must be kept in your bag.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn văn giới thiệu về bản thân dùng can/can't để nói về khả năng.
hint_words: I want to be able to
writing_prompts.example: Hello! My name is Linh and I'm twenty years old. I can speak Vietnamese and English, and I'm learning Korean. I can cook Vietnamese food well, but I can't cook Western food yet. I can swim and ride a bicycle, but I can't drive a car. I can draw and paint pictures in my free time. I want to be able to speak five languages one day. My goal is to travel around Asia and meet people from different countries.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết 5 câu hỏi dùng Can và trả lời chúng dựa trên khả năng thật của bạn.
writing_prompts.example:
Can you speak Japanese? – No, I can't, but I can speak English and Chinese.
Can I borrow your book? – Yes, you can. Please return it next week.
Can we meet at the park? – Yes, we can. What time works for you?
Can she play the piano? – Yes, she can. She plays beautifully.
Can he cook Vietnamese food? – No, he can't, but he's learning.

beginner.title: Imperative: Do / Don't / Let's
beginner.content:
📌 Khi nào dùng IMPERATIVE (Câu mệnh lệnh)
Dùng câu mệnh lệnh để:
• Ra lệnh / chỉ dẫn: Sit down. Open your books. Turn left.
• Đưa ra lời khuyên / gợi ý: Try this restaurant. Take an umbrella.
• Cảnh báo / ngăn cản: Be careful! Don't touch that!
• Đề nghị / mời: Have some more tea. Come in!
• Hướng dẫn / công thức: Mix the flour and eggs. Press the button.
📐 Công thức đầy đủ
LoạiCông thứcVí dụ
Khẳng địnhV (nguyên thể) + …Open the door. Be quiet. Listen carefully.
Phủ địnhDon't + V + …Don't run. Don't be late. Don't forget your keys.
Lịch sự hơnV + pleaseSit down, please. Pass the salt, please.
Let's (gợi ý)Let's + VLet's go! Let's have lunch. Let's not argue.
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ You open the window. → ✅ Open the window. (không cần chủ ngữ you)
❌ Don't to run in the corridor. → ✅ Don't run in the corridor. (Don't + V, không có to)
❌ Be not late. → ✅ Don't be late.
❌ Let's to go! → ✅ Let's go! (Let's + V nguyên thể, không có to)
Turn off your phone and sit down quietly, please.Tắt điện thoại và ngồi xuống yên lặng, làm ơn.
Don't leave your bags in the corridor — it's dangerous.Đừng để túi ở hành lang — nguy hiểm đấy.
Let's meet at the café at six and then decide what to do.Mình hẹn nhau ở quán cà phê lúc sáu rồi quyết định làm gì sau nhé.

exercises.q: ___ your homework before you watch TV tonight.
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh khẳng định → V nguyên thể (Do)

exercises.q: ___ run in the school corridor — it's dangerous.
___ go to the beach this afternoon — the weather is perfect!
___ the door when you leave — it gets very cold in here.
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh → V nguyên thể (Close)

exercises.q: ___ late for the interview — first impressions matter.
exercises.opts: Don't be,Not be,Be not,Don't to be
exercises.exp: Phủ định của be → Don't be

exercises.q: ___ the instructions carefully before you start the exam.
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh → V nguyên thể (Read)

exercises.q: Come in and ___ down, please. I'll be with you in a moment.
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh → V nguyên thể (sit)

exercises.q: ___ touch that — it's very fragile and expensive.
exercises.exp: Cảnh báo phủ định → Don't + V

exercises.q: ___ not argue — let's try to find a solution together.
exercises.exp: Let's not + V = gợi ý phủ định

exercises.q: ___ quiet in the library — other people are trying to study.
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh với be → Be + adjective

exercises.q: ___ forget to bring your passport to the airport tomorrow.
exercises.exp: Nhắc nhở phủ định → Don't + V

exercises.q: ___ the flour and eggs together, then add the milk slowly.
___ have some more cake — I made it this morning!
___ left at the traffic lights and then go straight on.
___ worry — everything will be fine. I promise.
___ more water every day — it's very good for your health.
Don't to run in the corridor. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: Don't run in the corridor
exercises.exp: Don't + V nguyên thể, không có 'to'

exercises.q: Let's go for a walk after dinner. → Correct or Wrong?
Be not late for the meeting. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: Don't be late for the meeting
exercises.exp: Phủ định mệnh lệnh với be → Don't be

exercises.q: Open the window, please — it's very hot in here. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.exp: V nguyên thể + please = lịch sự ✔

exercises.q: ___ (sit) down and ___ (open) your textbooks to page forty-five.
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh → V nguyên thể

exercises.q: ___ (not/talk) while the teacher is explaining. It's rude.
___ (be) patient — good things take time.
exercises.exp: Mệnh lệnh với be → Be + adjective

exercises.q: ___ (let's/go) somewhere different tonight — I'm bored of this place.
___ (not/forget) to lock the door when you leave the house.
___ (turn) right at the roundabout and then ___ (take) the first left.
___ (not/be) shy — just introduce yourself and smile.
___ (add) the sugar gradually and ___ (stir) until dissolved.
exercises.exp: Hướng dẫn nấu ăn → V nguyên thể

exercises.q: ___ (let's/not/argue) about this — we can discuss it calmly.
exercises.exp: Let's not + V = gợi ý phủ định

exercises.q: ___ (look) both ways before you cross the road. Always.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên an toàn → V nguyên thể

exercises.q: ___ (not/use) your phone during the film — it disturbs others.
___ (help) yourself to the food — there's plenty for everyone.
___ (not/eat) so much sugar — it's bad for your teeth.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên phủ định → Don't + V

exercises.q: ___ (press) the green button to start the machine.
___ (let's/try) that new Vietnamese restaurant on the corner tonight.
___ (be) careful when you cross that bridge — the surface is slippery.
___ (not/leave) your valuables on the table in a public place.
___ (write) your name and the date at the top of the page first.
___ (let's/take) a break — we've been working for three hours straight.
___ (not/worry) about the exam — you've prepared very well.

reorder.words: Sit down and open your books to page ten
Don't run in the corridor it is dangerous
Let's go for a walk after dinner tonight
Be quiet in the library other people are studying
Don't forget to bring your umbrella tomorrow morning
Turn left at the traffic lights and go straight on
Don't be late for the meeting it starts at nine
Mix the flour and eggs and then add the milk
Let's not argue let's find a solution together
Read the instructions carefully before you begin the test
Don't touch that it is very fragile and expensive
Drink more water every day it is good for you
Don't use your phone during the lesson please
Press the green button to start the machine
Let's try that new restaurant on the corner
Be careful when you cross the road at night
Don't leave your bag on the seat next to you
Write your name and the date at the top
Help yourself to the food there is plenty for everyone
Don't worry about the exam you will do well

passage.text: Welcome to the English Language Centre! Please {{1}} (read) these instructions carefully before your first class. {{2}} (arrive) five minutes early so the lesson can start on time. {{3}} (not/use) your mobile phone during class — it disturbs everyone. {{4}} (bring) a notebook and a pen to every session. {{5}} (not/be) afraid to make mistakes — that is how we all learn. {{6}} (ask) questions whenever you do not understand something. At break time, {{7}} (help) yourself to tea and coffee in the lounge. {{8}} (not/leave) your personal items unattended in the building. After class, {{9}} (complete) your homework before the next session. Finally, {{10}} (enjoy) your time here and {{11}} (not/worry) — you are going to make great progress!

Here is a simple recipe for a classic tomato pasta. First, {{1}} (boil) a large pot of salted water. {{2}} (add) the pasta and {{3}} (cook) for ten minutes. While the pasta cooks, {{4}} (heat) some olive oil in a pan. {{5}} (not/use) too much oil — just two tablespoons. {{6}} (add) the garlic and {{7}} (fry) for one minute. {{8}} (not/let) the garlic burn. Then {{9}} (pour) in the tomatoes and {{10}} (season) with salt and pepper. {{11}} (let's/taste) the sauce and adjust as needed. Finally, {{12}} (drain) the pasta and {{13}} (mix) it with the sauce. {{14}} (serve) immediately and {{15}} (not/forget) to add fresh basil on top!

{{1}} (listen) carefully — this is important safety information for the building. In case of fire, {{2}} (not/use) the lifts. {{3}} (use) the emergency stairs instead. {{4}} (stay) calm and {{5}} (follow) the signs to the nearest exit. {{6}} (not/run) — {{7}} (walk) quickly and calmly. Once outside, {{8}} (go) to the assembly point in the car park. {{9}} (not/re-enter) the building until the fire service says it is safe. {{10}} (check) that all your colleagues are present. If someone is missing, {{11}} (tell) the fire marshal immediately. {{12}} (not/worry) about your personal belongings — they can wait.

Before you start your morning run, {{1}} (do) a five-minute warm-up. {{2}} (stretch) your legs and arms carefully. {{3}} (not/run) too fast at the beginning — {{4}} (start) slowly and build up your pace. {{5}} (drink) some water before you leave the house. {{6}} (not/forget) to wear comfortable shoes with good support. During your run, {{7}} (breathe) steadily and {{8}} (not/hold) your breath. If you feel pain, {{9}} (stop) immediately and {{10}} (rest). After the run, {{11}} (cool) down gradually. {{12}} (not/sit) down straight away — {{13}} (walk) slowly for a few minutes first. Finally, {{14}} (drink) plenty of water and {{15}} (eat) something nutritious within an hour.

Welcome to the national park! {{1}} (enjoy) your visit but please follow these simple rules. {{2}} (stay) on the marked paths at all times. {{3}} (not/pick) flowers or plants — {{4}} (leave) them for others to enjoy. {{5}} (take) all your litter home with you. {{6}} (not/feed) the wild animals — it is dangerous for them and for you. {{7}} (keep) dogs on a lead at all times. If you see other visitors, {{8}} (be) considerate and {{9}} (keep) noise levels down. {{10}} (not/light) fires anywhere in the park. In an emergency, {{11}} (call) the park warden on the number shown on your map. {{12}} (have) a wonderful time!

writing_prompts.vi: Viết các quy tắc cho lớp học dùng câu mệnh lệnh khẳng định và phủ định.
writing_prompts.example: Here are the rules for our class. Arrive on time and sit in your place quietly. Don't talk when the teacher is speaking. Turn off your phone and put it away. Bring your textbook and notebook every day. Don't be rude to your classmates. Ask questions if you don't understand. Be polite and respect everyone.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết hướng dẫn cách làm một món ăn hoặc đồ uống đơn giản dùng câu mệnh lệnh.
writing_prompts.example: Here is how to make a simple omelette. First, break two eggs into a bowl and add a pinch of salt. Beat them well. Heat a little butter in a pan — don't let it burn. Pour in the eggs and cook on low heat. Don't stir too much. When the edges are firm, fold it in half. Serve immediately with toast.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết lời khuyên cho người bạn đang chuẩn bị cho một kỳ thi dùng câu mệnh lệnh.
writing_prompts.example: Don't leave everything until the last minute. Make a revision plan and stick to it. Study a little every day — don't try to learn everything in one night. Get enough sleep — don't stay up all night before the exam. Eat a good breakfast on the day. Don't panic if you don't know an answer — move on and come back to it.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết các hướng dẫn an toàn hoặc quy tắc cho một địa điểm (bể bơi, thư viện, bảo tàng).
writing_prompts.example: Welcome to the swimming pool. Please follow these safety rules. Don't run near the pool — the floor is very slippery. Always shower before entering the water. Don't dive in the shallow end. Keep the changing rooms tidy and don't leave your belongings on the floor. Be considerate of other swimmers. Don't shout or splash other people. Children — stay in the children's area at all times.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết các đề nghị và gợi ý dùng Let's và câu mệnh lệnh lịch sự.
writing_prompts.example: Let's meet at the park at ten and then decide what to do. Let's not take the motorway — the traffic is always terrible at the weekend. Come in and sit down — make yourself comfortable. Have some tea or coffee — help yourself. Let's try that new café everyone is talking about. Don't worry about the bill — let's split it equally.

beginner.title: Would you like… / I'd like…
beginner.content:
📌 Would like – Muốn / Muốn mời ai đó
Would like được dùng để:
• Diễn đạt mong muốn lịch sự (= want, nhưng lịch sự hơn):
I would like a coffee. / I'd like to speak to the manager.
• Mời hoặc đề nghị lịch sự:
Would you like some tea? / Would you like to come in?
• Hỏi về sở thích lịch sự:
What would you like to eat? / Where would you like to sit?
📐 Công thức đầy đủ
DạngCấu trúcVí dụ
+ danh từwould like + nounI'd like a glass of water.
+ động từwould like + to + VI'd like to book a table.
Câu hỏiWould + S + like + noun/to+V?Would you like some help? / Would you like to sit down?
Rút gọnI'd / you'd / he'd / she'd…She'd like to leave early today.
✅ So sánh: want vs would like
Want = thông thường / trực tiếp: I want a coffee. (trong gia đình, bạn bè)
Would like = lịch sự hơn: I'd like a coffee, please. (nhà hàng, người lạ, tình huống formal)
Would you like…? = mời lịch sự → Do you want…? = thông thường
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ I would like go. → ✅ I would like to go. (would like + to + V)
❌ Would you like going? → ✅ Would you like to go? (không dùng -ing sau would like)
❌ I'd like that you come. → ✅ I'd like you to come. (would like + O + to + V)
❌ She would likes a tea. → ✅ She would like a tea. (would không chia)
I'd like a window seat, please. Can you arrange that?Tôi muốn một chỗ ngồi cạnh cửa sổ. Bạn có thể sắp xếp không?
Would you like to try some of this cake? I made it myself.Bạn có muốn thử một ít bánh không? Tôi tự làm đấy.
She'd like to speak to someone about the complaint she made last week.Cô ấy muốn nói chuyện với ai đó về khiếu nại tuần trước.

exercises.q: I ___ a cup of tea, please. No sugar.
exercises.exp: Yêu cầu lịch sự → I'd like + noun

exercises.q: Would you like ___ a seat while you wait?
She ___ to speak to the manager about her order.
exercises.exp: would like + to + V (would không chia)

exercises.q: Would you like some more coffee? – Yes, ___.
exercises.exp: Short answer: Yes, I would. (hoặc: Yes, please.)

exercises.q: What ___ you like to eat this evening?
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi lịch sự → What would you like…?

exercises.q: They ___ to book two rooms for next weekend.
___ you like to come in and sit down?
exercises.exp: Mời lịch sự → Would you like to…?

exercises.q: I'd like ___ more information about the course, please.
He ___ like to apologise for any inconvenience caused.
exercises.exp: would like = modal, dùng would (không chia)

exercises.q: Would you like ___ or tea?
I'd like you ___ this report by end of day, please.
___ they like to join us for dinner tonight?
We ___ to thank everyone for their hard work this year.
Would you like some help with your luggage? – No, ___.
exercises.exp: Lịch sự từ chối → No, thank you.

exercises.q: I'd like ___ window seat on the right side of the plane.
She'd like to ___ to Paris for their anniversary.
exercises.exp: would like to + V nguyên thể

exercises.q: I would like go to the cinema tonight. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: I would like to go to the cinema
exercises.exp: would like + to + V nguyên thể

exercises.q: Would you like some tea or coffee? → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.exp: Would you like + some + noun = mời lịch sự ✔

exercises.q: She would likes a table for two, please. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: She would like a table for two
exercises.exp: would không chia theo chủ ngữ — luôn là would like

exercises.q: I'd like to book a room for three nights. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.exp: I'd like to + V nguyên thể ✔

exercises.q: I ___ a table for four people tonight at eight, please.
exercises.exp: Đặt chỗ lịch sự → I'd like + noun

exercises.q: ___ you like to sit inside or outside on the terrace?
exercises.exp: Mời lịch sự → Would you like to…?

exercises.q: She ___ to speak to someone about her travel insurance claim.
Would you like some more pasta? – Yes, ___, thank you.
exercises.exp: Chấp nhận lịch sự → Yes, please. / Yes, I would.

exercises.q: What ___ you like to do after the concert finishes?
They ___ to apologise for the delay with your order.
I ___ some information about your evening courses, please.
___ you like me to carry that heavy bag for you?
exercises.exp: Would you like + O + to + V = đề nghị giúp

exercises.q: He ___ to make a complaint about the service he received.
Would you like ___ tea or would you prefer coffee?
I ___ you to read this document carefully before you sign it.
___ they like to reserve a room with a sea view?
We ___ to thank all our customers for their continued support.
Would you like to ___ (try) the new menu? It's just launched.
exercises.exp: Would you like to + V nguyên thể

exercises.q: I ___ a window seat if one is available, please.
___ you like some help with the application form?
exercises.exp: Would you like some + noun = đề nghị giúp

exercises.q: She ___ to visit Italy next year for her birthday.
Would you like ___ (join) us for the welcome dinner on Friday?
exercises.exp: Would you like to + V nguyên thể

exercises.q: I ___ to make a reservation for two adults and one child.
___ you like me to explain the procedure again more slowly?
exercises.exp: Would you like + O + to + V

reorder.words: I'd like a table for two please
Would you like to sit inside or outside
She'd like to speak to the manager please
Would you like some more tea or coffee
What would you like to do this evening
I'd like some information about the evening classes
Would you like me to carry that for you
He'd like to make a complaint about the service
We'd like to book two rooms for next weekend
Would you like to try the new menu tonight
I'd like a window seat if one is available
She'd like to visit Japan for her next holiday
Would they like to join us for dinner
I'd like you to finish this report by Friday
Would you like some help with your luggage
I'd like to apologise for the inconvenience caused
Would you like a receipt for your purchase
She'd like to learn a new language next year
Would you like me to explain the rules again
I'd like to thank everyone for coming tonight

passage.text:
Waiter: Good evening! {{1}} a table by the window? There is one available.
Customer: Yes, please. That {{2}} perfect.
Waiter: {{3}} to see our menu?
Customer: Yes, I {{4}}. I {{5}} the fish, please. And {{6}} a glass of still water.
Waiter: Of course. {{7}} your partner {{8}} to order too?
Customer: She {{9}} the vegetarian pasta. And {{10}} we see the wine list?
Waiter: Certainly. {{11}} me to explain the daily specials first?

Receptionist: Good morning! How can I help you today?
Guest: Hello. I {{1}} to check in, please. My name is Johnson.
Receptionist: Of course, Mr Johnson. {{2}} a room with a city view or a garden view?
Guest: I {{3}} a city view if possible.
Receptionist: Certainly. {{4}} me to explain the hotel facilities?
Guest: Yes, please. I {{5}} to know about the gym and the spa.
Receptionist: Both are available from seven in the morning. {{6}} an early wake-up call tomorrow?
Guest: Yes, I {{7}}. At six thirty, please.
Receptionist: Done. {{8}} some help with your luggage?
Guest: No, thank you. But I {{9}} to know where the restaurant is.
Receptionist: It's on the ground floor. {{10}} a map of the hotel?

Hi everyone, and welcome to the orientation day. I {{1}} to start by thanking you all for coming. We {{2}} to give you a brief overview of our company. First, {{3}} everyone {{4}} to introduce themselves? Great. After the presentation, we {{5}} to take you on a tour of the building. {{6}} any of you {{7}} some refreshments before we begin? There is coffee and juice available. We {{8}} to finish by lunchtime and then we {{9}} you to join us for lunch in the canteen. {{10}} any questions before we start? Please don't hesitate — we {{11}} to answer everything today.

Travel agent: Good afternoon! {{1}} to book a holiday or are you just looking for ideas?
Customer: I {{2}} some help planning a trip to Thailand.
Travel agent: Wonderful! When {{3}} you {{4}} to travel?
Customer: We {{5}} to go in March if possible. There are two of us.
Travel agent: Perfect. {{6}} a beach resort or a city break?
Customer: We {{7}} a mix of both — some beach time and some sightseeing.
Travel agent: Great. {{8}} me to show you some options?
Customer: Yes, please. We {{9}} to stay in a nice hotel — not too expensive.
Travel agent: Of course. {{10}} to see some photos of the properties?
Customer: Yes, we {{11}}. That would be really helpful.

Doctor: Good morning. Please sit down. What {{1}} me to help you with today?
Patient: Thank you. I {{2}} to talk about some symptoms I've been having.
Doctor: Of course. {{3}} to describe them for me?
Patient: Yes. I {{4}} you to look at my throat — it has been very sore.
Doctor: Certainly. {{5}} to lie down on the bed over there?
Patient: Yes, please. I {{6}} to ask about some medication as well.
Doctor: Of course. {{7}} some information about possible side effects?
Patient: Yes, I {{8}}. I {{9}} to understand everything clearly.
Doctor: I understand. {{10}} me to write everything down for you?
Patient: That {{11}} be very helpful, thank you.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết hội thoại trong nhà hàng giữa khách và nhân viên phục vụ dùng would like.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Would like – ordering in a restaurant
writing_prompts.example:
Waiter: Good evening. Would you like to see the menu?
Customer: Yes, please. I'd like the pasta and a glass of red wine.
Waiter: Would you like a starter?
Customer: Yes, I'd like the soup, please.
Waiter: Would your friend like to order too?
Customer: She'd like the salmon and some sparkling water.
Waiter: Would you like any dessert later?
Customer: We'd like to see the dessert menu after the main course.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết email đặt phòng khách sạn dùng would like.
writing_prompts.example:
Dear Sir or Madam,
I'd like to book a room at your hotel for two nights, from the 15th to the 17th of March. I'd like a double room with a sea view if possible. I'd also like to know if breakfast is included. My wife and I would like to arrive early — around midday. Would it be possible to arrange an early check-in? I'd like to receive confirmation by email.
Thank you.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những điều bạn muốn làm trong tương lai dùng I'd like to.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: I'd like to – wishes and aspirations
writing_prompts.example: There are lots of things I'd like to do in the future. I'd like to travel to Japan and learn the language. I'd also like to study photography — I'd like to take better pictures. One day I'd like to open my own café. I wouldn't like to work in an office all my life — I'd like a job that keeps me moving.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết cuộc hội thoại tại quầy lễ tân khách sạn dùng would like.
writing_prompts.example:
Receptionist: Good afternoon! Would you like to check in?
Guest: Yes, please. I'd like to check in — my name is Nguyen.
Receptionist: Of course. Would you like a room with a city view?
Guest: I'd like a garden view if possible, please.
Receptionist: Certainly. Would you like an early morning call?
Guest: Yes please, at seven. I'd also like to know where the restaurant is.
Receptionist: It's on the second floor. Would you like some help with your luggage?

writing_prompts.vi: Viết 5 câu hỏi dùng Would you like và trả lời chúng một cách lịch sự.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Would you like…? – offers and invitations
writing_prompts.example:
Would you like some more tea? – Yes, please. That would be lovely.
Would you like to join us for lunch? – I'd love to, thank you.
Would you like some help with that? – No, thank you — I can manage.
Would you like to sit down? – Yes, please. I'm quite tired.
Would you like me to explain again? – Yes, I would. I didn't quite follow.

beginner.title: Verbs + -ing / to + Infinitive
beginner.content:
📌 Động từ theo sau bởi -ing hoặc to + V
Một số động từ theo sau bởi -ing, một số theo sau bởi to + V (infinitive), và một số theo sau bởi cả hai.
Không có quy tắc cứng — bạn cần học thuộc từng nhóm!
📋 Động từ + -ing (Gerund)
Động từVí dụ
enjoyShe enjoys reading novels.
finishHave you finished eating?
love / like / hateI love swimming. He hates waiting.
mindDo you mind opening the window?
suggestShe suggested going by train.
keepHe kept talking during the film.
stopShe stopped smoking last year.
avoidTry to avoid eating too much sugar.
📋 Động từ + to + V (Infinitive)
Động từVí dụ
wantI want to leave early.
needShe needs to study more.
decideHe decided to quit his job.
hopeI hope to see you soon.
planWe plan to travel next year.
learnShe is learning to drive.
forget / rememberDon't forget to call. Remember to bring your passport.
tryTry to be on time. (cố gắng)
📋 Động từ + -ing hoặc to + V (nghĩa không đổi ở A1)
love / like / hate / prefer + -ing hoặc to + V (ở A1, nghĩa gần giống nhau)
I love swimming. = I love to swim.  |  She hates waiting. = She hates to wait.
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ She enjoys to read. → ✅ She enjoys reading.
❌ I want going home. → ✅ I want to go home.
❌ He finished to eat. → ✅ He finished eating.
❌ They decided leaving. → ✅ They decided to leave.
❌ Do you mind to open the window? → ✅ Do you mind opening the window?
She enjoys cooking at the weekend but hates washing up afterwards.Cô ấy thích nấu ăn cuối tuần nhưng ghét rửa bát sau đó.
He decided to leave the company after ten years and start his own business.Anh ấy quyết định rời công ty sau 10 năm và tự mở doanh nghiệp.
Do you mind closing the window? It's getting cold in here.Bạn có phiền đóng cửa sổ không? Trời đang trở lạnh rồi.

exercises.q: She enjoys ___ to music while she cooks dinner in the evening.
He decided ___ a new course to improve his English skills.
Do you mind ___ the window? It's very cold in here.
She needs ___ harder if she wants to pass the exam.
I hate ___ in long queues — it wastes so much time.
They finished ___ the project just before the midnight deadline.
He kept ___ the same mistake even after the teacher corrected him.
She suggested ___ the earlier train to avoid the rush hour.
Don't forget ___ your passport when you travel internationally.
exercises.exp: forget + to + V (nhắc nhở tương lai)

exercises.q: I love ___ at the weekend when the weather is nice.
He is learning ___ the guitar — he has lessons every Friday.
She avoided ___ the subject because she knew it would cause an argument.
We plan ___ to Spain for two weeks this August.
He stopped ___ three years ago and his health improved dramatically.
Try ___ on time — the teacher gets annoyed when people are late.
I hope ___ you at the conference next month if I can get a ticket.
She enjoys to cook Italian food at the weekend. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: She enjoys cooking Italian food

exercises.q: He decided to quit his job and travel around Asia. → Correct or Wrong?
I want going to the cinema tonight — are you free? → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: I want to go to the cinema

exercises.q: Do you mind opening the door? My hands are full. → Correct or Wrong?
She loves ___ (cook) new recipes she finds on cooking websites.
He decided ___ (leave) early to avoid the traffic on the motorway.
Do you mind ___ (turn) down the music? I'm trying to concentrate.
They finished ___ (paint) the kitchen just before the guests arrived.
I need ___ (buy) a new laptop — this one is too slow.
She kept ___ (interrupt) me during my presentation, which was annoying.
We plan ___ (visit) my grandparents this summer if the weather is good.
He suggested ___ (take) a taxi instead of walking in the rain.
Don't forget ___ (call) the dentist to confirm your appointment tomorrow.
I hate ___ (wait) at airports — I find it really stressful.
She is learning ___ (drive) — she has her test in three months.
Try ___ (arrive) at least ten minutes before the interview starts.
He avoided ___ (make) eye contact during the difficult conversation.
I hope ___ (travel) to South America one day — it looks incredible.
She stopped ___ (eat) junk food and started going to the gym regularly.
He wants ___ (become) a doctor when he finishes school.
I enjoy ___ (read) historical novels — I always learn something new.
Remember ___ (lock) the back door before you go to bed tonight.
exercises.exp: remember + to + V (nhắc nhở tương lai)

exercises.q: They decided ___ (cancel) the trip because of the bad weather forecast.
She doesn't mind ___ (work) late if it means the project gets finished.

reorder.words: She enjoys reading novels in the evenings
He decided to leave the company after ten years
Do you mind closing the window please
They finished painting the house before the weekend
I need to buy some new shoes for the wedding
She kept making the same mistake in her essays
We plan to travel to Europe next summer
He suggested taking the bus instead of walking
Don't forget to bring your passport to the airport
I love cycling in the park at the weekend
She is learning to drive she has lessons on Fridays
Try to be on time the teacher gets annoyed
He avoided talking about the problem for weeks
I hope to see you at the conference next month
She stopped eating meat two years ago
He wants to become a teacher when he finishes university
I hate waiting in long queues at the supermarket
Remember to lock the door before you go to bed
They decided to cancel the event because of the rain
She doesn't mind working late if it helps the team

passage.text: My cousin Leo has always loved {{1}} (travel). Last year, he decided {{2}} (quit) his office job and spend six months exploring South-East Asia. His friends suggested {{3}} (plan) the trip carefully, but Leo preferred {{4}} (be) spontaneous. He avoided {{5}} (book) everything in advance. He kept {{6}} (meet) interesting people along the way. At first, he struggled {{7}} (adapt) to the heat, but he learned {{8}} (manage) it. He finished {{9}} (write) a travel blog about the whole experience when he returned. Now he hopes {{10}} (inspire) others to do the same. He needs {{11}} (find) a new job soon, but he doesn't regret {{12}} (take) the trip at all.

My friend Sara recently decided {{1}} (learn) a new language. She chose Japanese and started {{2}} (attend) an evening class at the local college. At first, she hated {{3}} (memorise) all the different characters. She kept {{4}} (forget) the vocabulary too. Her teacher suggested {{5}} (watch) Japanese films with subtitles at home. Sara enjoyed {{6}} (do) this because it felt less like studying. She avoided {{7}} (translate) everything and tried {{8}} (guess) the meaning from context instead. After three months, she stopped {{9}} (worry) about making mistakes. She hopes {{10}} (visit) Japan next year. She also plans {{11}} (start) learning Korean afterwards — she loves {{12}} (study) languages.

Tom has always enjoyed {{1}} (cook), but last month he decided {{2}} (enter) a local cooking competition. He spent weeks {{3}} (practise) his signature dish. He avoided {{4}} (use) recipes he had never tried before. He kept {{5}} (ask) his family to taste his food and give feedback. His sister suggested {{6}} (add) more spices to the sauce, which really helped. On competition day, Tom tried {{7}} (stay) calm but his hands were shaking. He finished {{8}} (plate) his dish just in time. He hoped {{9}} (win) but he knew the competition was tough. In the end, he managed {{10}} (impress) the judges and came second. He plans {{11}} (enter) again next year and he doesn't mind {{12}} (spend) more time preparing.

Last year, our school decided {{1}} (start) a recycling programme. The head teacher suggested {{2}} (install) bins in every classroom. She hoped {{3}} (reduce) the amount of waste the school produced. At first, some students kept {{4}} (put) rubbish in the wrong bins. Teachers had to remind them {{5}} (separate) their waste correctly. The school avoided {{6}} (buy) single-use plastic for events. Students enjoyed {{7}} (design) posters to raise awareness. The school also finished {{8}} (plant) a small garden where they grow vegetables. They plan {{9}} (expand) the garden next term. Students learn {{10}} (take) responsibility for the environment. The programme has stopped {{11}} (feel) like a chore — now everyone loves {{12}} (take) part.

My grandmother loves {{1}} (tell) stories about her childhood. She remembers {{2}} (walk) two miles to school every morning. She enjoyed {{3}} (play) outside all day because there was no television. She hated {{4}} (do) chores, especially {{5}} (wash) the dishes. But she learned {{6}} (cook) at a young age and still loves it now. She decided {{7}} (move) to the city when she was twenty. She needed {{8}} (find) work, so she avoided {{9}} (be) too picky about jobs. She kept {{10}} (save) money until she could afford her own flat. She hopes {{11}} (write) a book about her life one day. She says young people should try {{12}} (appreciate) how different life was.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những hoạt động bạn thích và ghét làm dùng enjoy/love/hate + -ing.
writing_prompts.example: I love spending time outdoors — I especially enjoy hiking in the mountains at the weekend. I also enjoy cooking for my friends and trying new recipes. I hate waiting in queues — I find it really boring. I don't mind doing housework, but I hate ironing. I enjoy reading before bed — it helps me relax.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về kế hoạch và quyết định trong tương lai dùng decide/plan/hope/want + to + V.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: decide / plan / hope / want + to + V
writing_prompts.example: I've decided to change my routine this year. I plan to wake up earlier and exercise more. I want to learn a new skill — I hope to start a photography course next month. I need to save more money too. I've also decided to spend less time on social media and read more books instead.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những điều bạn tránh làm và ngừng làm dùng avoid/stop + -ing.
writing_prompts.example: I stopped eating fast food six months ago and I feel much healthier. I avoid going to the supermarket when I'm hungry — I always buy too much! I've stopped watching TV late at night because it was affecting my sleep. I try to avoid checking my phone first thing in the morning. I stopped buying coffee every day and started making it at home instead.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về bạn bè hoặc thành viên gia đình dùng nhiều cấu trúc V + -ing và V + to + V.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Mixed: V + -ing and V + to + V
writing_prompts.example: My brother loves playing video games but he decided to reduce his screen time this year. He suggested going to the gym together and we've been going twice a week. He plans to run a 10km race next spring and he enjoys training for it. He keeps forgetting to drink enough water, though! He wants to become a PE teacher one day and hopes to apply for a course next year.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết đoạn văn về thói quen học tiếng Anh dùng V + -ing và V + to + V.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: V + -ing and V + to + V – study habits
writing_prompts.example: I enjoy learning English — I find it really satisfying when I understand something new. I try to practise every day, even if it's just for twenty minutes. I avoid translating everything into my own language — I try to think in English instead. I keep a vocabulary notebook and I enjoy writing new words in it. I've decided to watch more films in English to improve my listening. I suggest trying this too — it really helps!
